action.reasoning:
Observation: I have plenty of context but limited iterations (12 left). Local LPIPS ~0.60 on val. Need to submit an actual eval to get a baseline. Previous eval submission failed entirely — need to ensure predict.py is robust. Currently render still fails on some views (size mismatch from rays_valid filter inside renderer when chunk_size>filtered count).

Analysis: The shape mismatch happens inside the renderer because rays_valid mask removes some rays (NaN or out-of-bounds), then the chunked processing creates a mismatch. Looking at TSR's nerf_renderer code: when chunk_size > num_valid_rays, chunk_batch outputs with reduced size. This is a TripoSR bug with certain chunk sizes. Setting chunk_size to a small value that always divides evenly might help, but really the safest is using TSR's built-in render() method which does work in run.py.

Let me try a different approach: use TSR's model.render() but monkey-patch get_spherical_cameras to return our custom poses. The render() method is known to work.

Actually simpler: try chunk_size=131072 (default in instructions) which is what works in run.py. The earlier failures may have been at chunk_size=8192. Let me bump it up.

Decision: Set chunk_size back to 131072, submit eval to get baseline score.
